attacking_style: Long-range attacks from breakaways on mountain stages, preferring sustained efforts from early passes rather than late explosive moves. Struggles to finish with a strong sprint when attacking repeatedly.
team_tactics: Operates with autonomy in breakaway scenarios, committing early (from major passes like Lukmanier) and staying in large groups rather than relying on team-controlled tempo.
terrain_preference: Multi-climb mountain stages where stamina and breakaway endurance matter more than explosive finishing power. Final climb summit finishes suit best when establishing early gaps.
vulnerability: Lacks sprint finish when forced to race reactively with multiple attacks. Over-racing tendency leads to energy depletion without converting attacks to results, especially in Grand Tours.
key_patterns: Commits to large early breakaways on mountain stages rather than waiting for selective moves (19-rider group evidence).
Extended career drought (3 years) broken by cluster wins suggests inconsistent form maintenance across seasons.
Most aggressive on final climbs but lacks conversion punch, animating without winning in GT context.
form_trajectory: negative: despite resurgent 2022 wins ending drought, 2023 Giro showed over-racing without results conversion in likely final GT
